返回本批次

Lichess与Take Take Take签署合作协议

Lichess and Take Take Take Sign Cooperation Agreement

lichess.org 作者 stevage 分数 177 评论 53 2026/04/09 19:56
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. Take Take Take 将使用 Lichess 作为其新游戏区的基础设施，以赢得开源关键细节：
  2. Take Take Take 将使用 Lichess 作为其新游戏区的基础设施，以赢得开源 Lichess 不会发生任何变化，它将保持一如既往 - 免费/自由和开源软件 Take Take Take 上的玩家现在可以轻松创建 Lichess 帐户，并通过 Take Take Take 应用程序在 Lichess 上玩游戏 我们所有的玩家（使用 Take Take Take 或使用 Lichess 网站或应用程序的玩家）都受益于 Lichess 承诺的一切 - 隐私、数据完整性和审核 Take Take Take 将通过直接和间接为 Lichess 做出贡献，包括在财务上和提高知名度，为开源做出贡献
  3. 今年早些时候，Take Take Take 向我们提出了一项独特的提案。 Take Take Take 要求我们分享我们的“数字共享资源”并使用我们的基础设施和游乐区，而不是从头开始构建自己的专有游乐区（“围墙花园”）。起初，我们谨慎地考虑了这个提议，甚至可能有些谨慎。 Lichess 经常被那些不了解我们或我们的价值观、只想利用甚至剥削我们的玩家和社区的公司所接触。但经过仔细审查并了解 Take Take Take 背后的团队后，我们认为这种合作有可能对整个国际象棋产生真正的积极影响。对于国际象棋爱好者和棋手来说，它代表了国际象棋生态系统内更健康的竞争。对于任何市场（包括国际象棋市场）而言，竞争和拥有更多选择本质上是一件好事。它迫使原本要作为股息支付或输送给机构投资者的资金重新投资到国际象棋中。这意味着，为了使服务脱颖而出，必须通过提供真正有用的新功能或安排大型锦标赛和活动来进行创新。这最终改善了向国际象棋社区和国际象棋玩家提供的服务，迫使所有在线平台加强他们的游戏。良性竞争和更加多元化的市场应该受到真正希望发展游戏的同行的欢迎。除了支持国际象棋领域的更多市场选择之外，我们还认为这是 Lichess 和整个免费/自由开源软件的重大胜利。他们没有像之前的几个昂贵的缺陷那样花费数百万美元建造和维护另一个围墙花园，而是意识到我们已经提供了免费且可用的数字共享空间，并询问是否可以帮助我们扩展它。通过选择我们的开源软件而不是构建自己的专有游戏区，这是对我们的软件已经成为市场上最好的软件的重大认可。这种模型在开源领域经常被采用。例如，各种 Linux 发行版都是开源的，并且是互联网的支柱，大多数 Web 服务器都运行 Linux。但即使该代码是开源的并且可以使用或分叉，但打包的是扩展和技术知识。同样，我们还提供我们的开发人员、基础设施、审核和一般运营方面的专业知识，他们在 15 年多的时间里一直在维护和改进 Lichess。使用 Take Take Take 玩的游戏将在 Lichess 上运行。新玩家，包括通过 Take Take Take 找到我们的玩家，将注册 Lichess 帐户，他们将在我们的服务器上玩游戏并使用我们的基础设施。他们将拥有我们为所有社区提供的相同数据权利和保护，以及我们相同的高水平审核。归根结底，它们是巫妖游戏，应该像对待巫妖游戏一样对待。 Lichess 不再只是一个游戏平台，而是成为免费在线国际象棋的基础设施层。与此同时，我们的独立性、我们的理念和价值观仍然是不容谈判的。 Lichess将永远保持自由和开放。我们的软件将始终保持开源。任何使用 Lichess 的游戏区域都必须始终免费，即使围绕它构建的其他工具并非如此。我们必须并且将会保证用户数据、用户隐私和审核行为的完整性。同样，Take Take Take 团队也非常理解这一点，并给了我们很大的开放性和透明度。他们明白，我们建立的东西最初是一个业余爱好网站，后来发展成为一个主要的全球平台，每天为数百万玩家提供服务，每秒即时传输数千个动作，为快速时间控制提供最佳体验。我们已经尽可能地进行了技术优化，同时也进行了组织优化，内容、广播、审核和管理的完成费用不到 Chess.com 预计年收入的 1%。他们了解我们所取得的成就，这就是为什么他们选择相信我们作为他们的游戏区。随着国际象棋市场变得越来越垄断、腐败，甚至从最初的愿景来看，我们也必须做出调整，以确保国际象棋界不会陷入困境并被利用来谋取私利。我们很感兴趣并渴望看到这种合作如何改变国际象棋生态系统内的事物，因为我们坚持我们的理念和价值观。虽然我们有时会感觉自己像商业化环境中的一个孤立的棋子，但当我们找到志同道合的盟友，他们想要最好的国际象棋并认识到我们的愿景时，总是令人耳目一新。现在，由于游戏的不可预测性，我们发现自己处于“Take Take Take”的棋子方阵中，全球社区也支持我们，并像我们一样相信国际象棋属于每个人。我们期待与您分享国际象棋更多的人，在一起。您也可以在他们的网站上从 Take Take Take 的角度阅读该公告。路透社___ 更新 - 2026 年 4 月 7 日虽然对此公告的反馈总体上是积极的，但我们社区的许多成员提出了我们希望解决的担忧。这些担忧是合理的，Lichess 团队的成员在讨论该合作协议时也提出了这些担忧 - 所以我们肯定听到了您的想法。 Lichess 团队的大部分成员都是无偿志愿者，他们为 Lichess 贡献了数百到数千个小时。请相信，我们也希望 Lichess 能得到最好的东西，并致力于其价值观。
  4. TTT是否只是利用Lichess的玩家群来启动他们自己的应用程序，并计划在未来运行自己的游戏区，从而有效地窃取Lichess玩家？这是Lichess团队中很多人最初关心的问题！从我们现在的角度来看，国际象棋平台生态系统中拥有更多“玩家”是一件好事，可以推动创新。对于我们来说，从战略上来说，有几种可能的路径：
  5. TTT 建立了自己的游戏区并且取得了成功——Lichess 挤在两个大型企业实体之间。 TTT 建立了自己的游戏区，但并不成功 - 现状。 TTT 与 chess.com 建立了一个游戏区 - 垄断进一步加剧，社区的选择有限。 TTT 与我们一起创建了一个游戏区并且取得了成功 - 开源的好处。 TTT 与我们建立了一个游戏区，但不成功 - 现状。

Original Summary

  1. Take Take Take will use Lichess as the infrastructure of their new play zone, in a win for open sourceKey details:
  2. Take Take Take will use Lichess as the infrastructure of their new play zone, in a win for open source No changes to Lichess, it will remain as it always has - free/libre and open-source software Players on Take Take Take will now be able to easily create Lichess accounts, and play games on Lichess through the Take Take Take app All of our players (those on Take Take Take or using the Lichess website or apps) benefit from everything Lichess promises - privacy, data integrity, and moderation Take Take Take will contribute to open source by contributing to Lichess directly and indirectly, including financially and with increased visibility
  3. Earlier this year, Take Take Take approached us with a unique proposal. Rather than building their own proprietary play zone from the ground up (a “walled garden”), Take Take Take have asked us to share our “digital commons” and use our infrastructure and play zone. At first, we considered the proposal with caution, and maybe even some wariness. Lichess has often been approached by companies who don’t understand us or our values, and just want to use or even exploit our players and community. But after careful scrutiny, and getting to know the team behind Take Take Take, we think this cooperation has the potential to be a real positive for chess as a whole. For chess enthusiasts and players, it represents more healthy competition within the chess ecosystem. Competition and having more choice is inherently a good thing for any market, including chess. It forces money that was originally destined to be paid out as dividends or funnelled up to institutional investors to be reinvested back into chess. It means that innovation by making genuinely useful new features, or arranging major tournaments and events, must occur in order for a service to stand out. This ultimately improves what’s on offer to the chess community and to chess players, forcing all online platforms to step up their game. Healthy competition and a more diverse market should be welcomed by our peers who truly wish to grow the game. Beyond supporting more market options within chess, we also view this as a major win for Lichess and for free/libre open-source software as a whole. Instead of Take Take Take building and maintaining another walled garden for millions of dollars, like several expensive foibles before them, they realised we already offer a free and available digital commons, and asked if they could help us expand it. By choosing our open-source software instead of building their own proprietary play zone, this is a significant recognition of our software already being the best in the market. This kind of model is adopted quite often in the open-source space. For example, various Linux distributions are open-source, and are the backbone of the internet with the majority of web servers running Linux. But even though that code is open-source and can be used or forked, it’s the scaling and technical know-how which is packaged. Similarly, we’re providing Take Take Take the expertise of our developers, infrastructure, moderation and general operations, who have been maintaining and improving Lichess as a whole for over 15 years. The games played using Take Take Take will be running on Lichess. New players, including those finding us via Take Take Take, will sign up for Lichess accounts, and they will play on our servers and use our infrastructure. They’ll have the same data rights and protections we give all of our community, and our same high level of moderation. Ultimately, they are Lichess games, being treated exactly like Lichess games should be treated. That’s Lichess becoming more than just a playing platform, but becoming an infrastructure layer for free online chess generally. At the same time, our independence, our philosophy and values remain non-negotiable. Lichess will always remain free and open. Our software will always remain open source. Any play zone using Lichess must always be free to play, even if other tools are built around it which are not. And we must and will have complete integrity of user data, user privacy, and moderation actions. Equally, the Take Take Take team have very much understood that, and given us a lot of openness and transparency. They understand that we’ve built something which began as a hobby site, and has grown into a major global platform, serving millions of players a day, instantaneously transmitting thousands of moves a second, giving the best experience for fast time controls. That we’ve taken technical optimisation as far as we can, but also organisational optimisation, with content, broadcasts, moderation, and administration being done on less than 1% of Chess.com's estimated annual revenue. They understand what we’ve achieved, and that’s why they’ve chosen to trust us to be their play zone. As the chess market has become increasingly monopolised, enshittified, and surely even corrupted from an original vision, we similarly must adapt to ensure the chess community isn’t trapped and exploited for private gain. We’re intrigued and keen to see how this cooperation changes things within the chess ecosystem, as we stay true to our philosophies and values. While we can sometimes feel like an isolated pawn in a commercialised landscape, it’s always refreshing when we find like-minded allies who want the best for chess and recognise our vision for that. Now, through the unpredictable nature of the game, we find ourselves in a pawn phalanx with Take Take Take, and a global community supporting us and believing, like us, that chess belongs to everyone. We’re looking forward to sharing chess with more people, together. You can read the announcement from Take Take Take's perspective, on their site also.Reuters ___ Update - 07-Apr-2026 While feedback to this announcement has broadly been positive, a number of members of our community have raised concerns that we want to address. These concerns are reasonable, and were also raised by members of the Lichess team during discussions about this cooperation agreement - so we definitely hear where you're coming from. The majority of the Lichess team are unpaid volunteers, who have given hundreds to thousands of hours to Lichess. Please believe that we also want what is best for Lichess and are committed to its values.
  4. Are TTT just using Lichess's playerbase to kickstart their own app, and planning to run their own play zone in the future, effectively stealing the Lichess players? This was the initial concern from many in the Lichess team! The way we see it now, having more "players" in the chess platform ecosystem is a good thing, driving innovation. For us, strategically, there were several possible paths:
  5. TTT makes their own play zone and is successful - Lichess squished between two large corporate entities. TTT makes their own play zone and is unsuccessful - status quo. TTT makes a play zone with chess.com - monopolisation increases further, with limited options for the community. TTT makes a play zone with us and is successful - open source benefits. TTT makes a play zone with us and is unsuccessful - status quo.

评论（默认中文）

TheRoque 2026/04/09 23:44
向巫妖王致以崇高的敬意。开源、无广告、超干净的界面和超强功能的网站。与它相比，Chess.com 使用起来很痛苦。

他们的所有财务状况也是公开的： https://lichess.org/costs

Huge respect to Lichess. Open source, no ads, super clean interface and super functional website. Chess.com is a pain to use compared to it.

All their finances are also public: https://lichess.org/costs

在 HN 中查看
sillysaurusx 2026/04/10 02:59
他们的首席开发人员的年薪为 7.2 万美元，移动开发人员的年薪为 4.9 万美元。

我不知道该怎么想，但这绝对很有趣。我想知道 chess.com 向他们的工程师支付的工资是多少。

Their lead developer is paid $72k/yr, and mobile developer $49k/yr.

I'm not sure what to think, but that's definitely interesting. I wonder what chess.com is paying their engineers.

在 HN 中查看
tumdum_ 2026/04/10 03:44
您能解释一下这些薪水中有趣的地方吗？
Can you explain what is interesting in those salaries?
在 HN 中查看
morcus 2026/04/10 04:03
它们非常低（至少按照美国软件工程师的标准）
They are extremely low (at least by the standard of Software Engineers based in the US)
在 HN 中查看
tumdum_ 2026/04/10 04:33
Lichess 是法国的一个非营利组织（就像它的作者一样），这些薪水对于法国来说很正常。
Lichess is a non profit from France (like it’s author) and those salaries quite normal for France.
在 HN 中查看
YetAnotherNick 2026/04/10 05:38
不，不是。是的，这可能适用于普通的日常开发人员，但如果有人可以单独运行拥有数百万活跃用户的网站，那么根据开发人员所在的位置，薪水没有差异。 Mistral 向研究人员支付 10 万美元的薪水吗？
No it is not. Yes it could be for your average everyday developer but if someone can run site with millions of active users alone, there is no difference in salary based on where the developer stays. Does Mistral pays $100k salary to researchers?
在 HN 中查看
tumdum_ 2026/04/10 09:19
这是“非”利润，所以我认为他给自己付钱是完全正常的。这份收入不错，可以让他过上正常舒适的生活。

米斯特拉尔是“为了”利润。

It’s a *non* profit so I think it’s perfectly normal that he pays himself that. It’s a good salary which lets him live normal comfortable life.

Mistral is *for* profit.

在 HN 中查看
victorbjorklund 2026/04/10 05:53
很确定 OpenAI 的薪水比 chess.com 高
Pretty sure OpenAI pays higher than chess.com
在 HN 中查看
YetAnotherNick 2026/04/10 06:13

OpenAI doesn't pay 1 developer higher salary than all the chess.com developers combined.

solarized 2026/04/10 09:12
纯粹为了好玩的产品和以金钱为导向的产品之间存在巨大差异。
A huge difference between just for fun and money oriented products.

sebmellen 2026/04/10 03:12
可以兼职
Could be part time
在 HN 中查看
static_motion 2026/04/10 03:59
或全职。在一些国家，这些工资相当不错。我在一个较贫穷的欧洲国家赚了 5 万欧元，这使我跻身前 8%。
Or full time. In some countries, those are pretty decent salaries. I earn €50k in one of the poorer European countries and that puts me in the top ~8%.

sebmellen 2026/04/10 08:22

在 HN 中查看
sillysaurusx 2026/04/10 03:15
我也这么认为，但他们表中的另一个条目是“系统管理员（兼职）”，年费为 18,000 美元。因此，要么他们忘记把兼职放在括号里，要么他们领取全职工资。我想知道哪个...
I thought so too, but another entry in their sheet is "Sysadmin (part time)" for $18k/yr. So either they forgot to put part time in parens, or they're paid full time wages. I wonder which...
在 HN 中查看
DEDLINE 2026/04/10 01:45
100%。美丽的财产。谢谢你，巫妖，为你所做的一切。
100%. Beautiful property. Thank you, Lichess, for everything you do.
在 HN 中查看
elAhmo 2026/04/10 01:22
令人惊讶的是，“法国社会保障/养老金贡献”几乎与他们的服务器总成本相同，而且有很多。

在只有少数员工的情况下，与 Lichess 等每月有 5-1000 万用户使用的服务相比，比较其中一些贡献的成本是多少（实际上只影响一两个人）是非常有趣的。

Quite amazing to see that "French social security / pension contrib" are almost the same as their total server costs, and there is loads of them.

With just a few employees, it is quite interesting to compare how much do some of these contributions cost, effectively affecting only a person or two, compared to a service like Lichess which is used by 5-10 million of users each month.

在 HN 中查看
lxgr 2026/04/10 01:21
这确实是非凡的。

即使是我的钻石白金极限 chess.com 订阅（或者被称为十几个级别中第三好的级别）的功能也比 Lichess 的唯一级别要少得多。

It's truly phenomenal.

Even my diamond platinum extreme chess.com subscription (or however the third-best tier of a dozen or so is called) has much less functionality than Lichess's only tier.

在 HN 中查看
cjpearson 2026/04/09 23:26
Take Take Take 上的链接帖子很有趣。马格努斯·卡尔森创建了 chess.com 的竞争对手，并最终将其出售给 chess.com 并成为赞助商。在担任赞助商期间，他创建了一个新的 chess.com 竞争对手。

我是 Lichess 的赞助人，很高兴看到他们得到支持，但在这种情况下，我确实为 chess.com 感到有点难过。马格努斯是国际象棋界的重要人物，国际棋联和 chess.com 等组织认为他们别无选择，只能满足他的突发奇想，但这并没有任何保证。我希望如果马格努斯决定“改变交易”，利切斯不会发现自己处于不利的境地。

The linked post on Take Take Take is interesting. Magnus Carlsen created a chess.com competitor and eventually sold it to chess.com and became a sponsor. While working as a sponsor he then created a new chess.com competitor.

I'm a Lichess patron and happy to see them get support, but I do feel a bit bad for chess.com in this case. Magnus is such a big figure in chess that organizations like FIDE and chess.com feel they have no choice but to accommodate his whims, but that doesn't come with any guarantees. I hope Lichess does not find themselves in a poor position if Magnus decides to "alter the deal".

在 HN 中查看
lxgr 2026/04/10 01:25
> 我确实对 chess.com 感觉有点不好

我确信他们会一路哭到银行。

> 我希望如果马格努斯决定“改变交易”，利切斯不会发现自己处于不利的境地。

我也希望他们能够避免依赖这笔交易赋予他们的一切。

> I do feel a bit bad for chess.com

I'm sure they'll be crying all the way to the bank.

> I hope Lichess does not find themselves in a poor position if Magnus decides to "alter the deal".

I also hope they manage to avoid becoming dependent on whatever this deal grants them.

在 HN 中查看
randomNumber7 2026/04/10 03:13
> 国际棋联和 chess.com 觉得他们别无选择，只能满足他的突发奇想

FIDE 和 chess.com 有时确实表现得很糟糕，我认为马格努斯有能力对抗他们。

> FIDE and chess.com feel they have no choice but to accommodate his whims

FIDE and chess.com did behave pretty shitty sometimes and I think its good Magnus is in a position to counter them a bit.

在 HN 中查看
kelipso 2026/04/10 03:35
我不知道。在这种情况下，每个人最终都会受到名人突发奇想的影响。
I don’t. Everyone ends up affected by the whims of a celebrity in this case.
在 HN 中查看
xiphias2 2026/04/10 01:13
他们有一个选择：学习国际象棋并击败马格努斯。在那之前我会更关心马格努斯（和巫妖）而不是那些生意。

他们做的最好的事情就是购买了一个令人惊叹的域名。

They have a choice: study chess and beat Magnus. Until then I will care about Magnus (and lichess) more then those businesses.

The best thing they did was that they bought an amazing domain name.

在 HN 中查看
jeremyjh 2026/04/10 01:12
马格努斯过去曾多次通过他拥有或参与的前任表示，他根本没有过多参与商业方面的工作；他主要是一个投资者和一个宣传演员。当然，没有经过他的同意，他们是不会这样做的。他也一直是 Lichess 的粉丝，参加了很多他们的比赛。
Magnus has said multiple times in the past - through the predecessors he owned or was involved with that he is not involved in the business side much at all; he's mostly an investor and a promotional actor. Of course they didn't do this without his agreement. He's always been a fan of Lichess too and played lots of their tournaments.
在 HN 中查看
Invictus0 2026/04/10 00:00
生意就是生意。竞业禁止期满。不要在 chess.com 上浪费你的感情。
Business is business. Non-competes expire. Don't waste your feelings on chess.com.
在 HN 中查看
senko 2026/04/10 03:02
来自 TTT：

> Take Take Take 的联合创始人马格努斯·卡尔森 (Magnus Carlsen) 不会在平台推出时积极推广该平台。 Take Take Take 现在提供完整的游戏和学习体验，但它进入了与他与 Chess.com 的大使协议相冲突的领域。他仍然是公司的联合创始人和最大股东，团队预计一旦这些合同限制发生变化，他的参与就会恢复。目前，产品必须自己说话。

From TTT:

> Magnus Carlsen, co-founder of Take Take Take, will not be actively promoting the platform at launch. With Take Take Take now offering a full play and learning experience, it enters territory that conflicts with his ambassador agreement with Chess.com. He remains a co-founder and the company's largest shareholder, and the team expects his involvement to resume once those contractual constraints change. For now, the product will have to speak for itself.

在 HN 中查看
SecretDreams 2026/04/10 00:00
当一个人对系统的结果产生过大的影响时，所有大型系统本质上都是脆弱的。解决方案不是希望马格努斯是无私的，而是不允许马格努斯（或任何个人）直接或通过他们的综合影响/追随者推动有意义的结果。
All large systems are inherently weak when one individual has an outsized influence on their outcomes. The solution is not to hope Magnus is altruistic, but to not allow Magnus (or any individual) to drive meaningful outcomes directly or through their combined influence/followings.
在 HN 中查看
jacobolus 2026/04/10 02:51
马格努斯“推动了有意义的成果”，因为他非常擅长国际象棋，而且公众喜欢看他下棋，因此各种与国际象棋相关的企业都会向他支付赞助费用。您如何建议“不允许他”产生这种影响？禁止在营销和产品中使用任何人的名字？
Magnus "drives meaningful outcomes" because he's really good at chess and members of the public enjoy watching him play, so various chess-related businesses will pay him money for sponsorship. How do you propose to "not allow him" that influence? Ban all use of people's names in marketing and products?
在 HN 中查看
SecretDreams 2026/04/10 03:14
我的评论超出了马格努斯的范围。回复：马格努斯。赞助还是不错的他赚钱就好了。他不应该能够决定游戏规则或游戏平台。国际海事组织。

他并不是第一个“真正擅长国际象棋”的人。

这是一个宽泛的说法，意思是“名人拥有太大的平台，对普通人的影响力太大”。

My commentary goes above and beyond just Magnus. Re: Magnus. Sponsorships are fine. Him making money is fine. He shouldn't be able to dictate the rules of the game or the platforms by which it is played. IMO.

He's not the first person to be "really good at chess".

It's a broad statement meant to mean "celebrities have too big of a platform and too much influence over the average joe".

在 HN 中查看
jacobolus 2026/04/10 10:06
马格努斯并没有“规定游戏规则或玩游戏的平台”，所以我不确定你的观点是什么。

您认为马格努斯应该被迫参加他不喜欢/不喜欢其形式的国际象棋比赛吗？或者您认为应该阻止 FIDE 或 chess.com 等组织试图吸引马格努斯参加他们的活动？

请说得更具体、更具体。

Magnus doesn't "dictate the rules of the game or the platforms by which it is played", so I'm not sure what your point is.

You think Magnus should be forced to participate in chess events he doesn't enjoy / doesn't like the format of? Or you think organizations like FIDE or chess.com should be blocked from trying to entice Magnus to participate in their events?

Please be more specific and concrete.

在 HN 中查看
sigbottle 2026/04/10 00:21
对于国际象棋世界、开源世界（BDFL）等来说可能还不错。对于现实世界的政府...
Well for the chess world, open source world (BDFL), etc probably okay. For real world governments...
在 HN 中查看
kelipso 2026/04/10 03:38
感谢您在这个话题上发出了罕见的明智声音。如今，国际象棋爱好者的狂热已经疯狂。我知道现在棋迷的平均年龄很低，大概14岁左右，但这仍然很烦人。
Thank you being a rare sensible voice on this topic. The fanboyism of chess fans is insane these days. I get that the average age of chess fans these days is very low, probably 14 or so, but it’s still pretty annoying.
在 HN 中查看
yewenjie 2026/04/09 23:08
我爱 Lichess 胜过一切，我希望这能为他们带来很多可以独立使用的捐款，并且 Lichess 品牌不会被 Take Take Take 和他们的公司资金所包含。
I love Lichess more than anything, and I hope this brings a lot of donation to them that they can use independently, and that the Lichess brand does not get subsumed by Take Take Take and their corporate money.
在 HN 中查看
sheiyei 2026/04/09 22:58
Lichess 的优化非常好 [0]（以及令人惊叹的公共服务）。我确信这对于 TTT 来说是非常划算的，所以是双赢的。

[0] https://lichess.org/@/revoof/blog/optimizing-the-tablebase-s...

Lichess is incredibly well optimized [0] (and an amazing public service). I'm sure that this is very cost effective for TTT, so a win-win.

[0] https://lichess.org/@/revoof/blog/optimizing-the-tablebase-s...

在 HN 中查看
Imustaskforhelp 2026/04/09 23:18
Lichess 是用 Scala 编写的，托管在专门的 OVH 上，费用非常少（我认为每月只需几千美元），并且托管了数百万玩家和游戏。

这是一种轻描淡写的说法，他们的优化程度取决于他们使用的优化技术和他们使用的基础设施提供商。即使在 AWS 等领域，同样的事情也可能带来更多的资金。

老实说，它还表明您基本上不需要 AWS/GCP/Azure 来完成所有事情。

Lichess是希望的灯塔，祝贺lichess团队与TTT的这次合作。

Lichess is written in Scala and is hosted on dedicated OVH for a very significantly small amount of money (I think just a few thousand dollars per month) and hosts so many millions of players and games.

It's an understatement how well optimized they are right down to the optimization techniques that they use and the infra providers that they use. The same thing even in something like AWS could cause significantly more amount of money.

It also shows that you don't need AWS/GCP/Azure for basically just about everything, to be honest.

Lichess is a beacon of hope and congrats to the lichess team for this cooperation with TTT.

在 HN 中查看
embedding-shape 2026/04/09 23:43
> 老实说，它还表明您基本上不需要 AWS/GCP/Azure 来完成所有事情。

这就是他们获胜的地方，人们认为 AWS/GCP/Azure 必须成为默认选择，而实际上，实际需要能够快速扩展/缩小的平台数量可能低于所有平台的 1%。大多数平台可以通过适当的专用硬件来节省资金并运行得更好，而不是默认使用云。

回想起我生命中的某个时刻，一个团队（成功）推动为一个应用程序构建分布式架构，我们甚至不知道它是否适合产品市场。快进 3 年后的今天，该应用程序不再在线，但虽然它有 5 个用户，但他们使用的是真正可靠的基础设施，我想这很酷。

> It also shows that you don't need AWS/GCP/Azure for basically just about everything, to be honest.

That's where they won, people think AWS/GCP/Azure has to be the default while in reality, the number of platforms that actually need to be able to scale up/down fast are probably below 1% of all platforms out there. Most platforms would save money and run better with proper dedicated hardware rather than going for clouds by default.

Flashback to a moment in my life where a team pushed (successfully) for building a distributed architecture for an app that we didn't even knew if it had product market fit yet. Fast forward 3 years to today and the app is no longer online, but while it had 5 users they were using really reliable infrastructure, I guess that's cool.

在 HN 中查看
lxgr 2026/04/10 01:29
正是同样的原则，让这么多人对那些比自己富裕得多的人的担忧产生了不合理的同情：统计上令人难以置信的假设是，他们也很快就会以某种方式成为 1% 的一部分。最好早点开始扮演这个角色！
It's the same principle that makes so many people unreasonably sympathetic to the concerns of those much wealthier than themselves: The statistically implausible assumption that they, too, will soon be part of the 1% somehow. Better start acting the part early!
在 HN 中查看
HWR_14 2026/04/10 00:12
根据云中的开发成本，这可能是正确的选择。当您没有适合市场的产品时，优化每个用户的成本可能是最糟糕的早期优化。
Depending on the development cost of being in the cloud, it was probably the right choice. Optimizing for cost per user when you don't have product market fit is probably the worse early optimization.
在 HN 中查看
hxtk 2026/04/10 02:52
拥有的云越少，云就越具有成本效益，因为维护一个具有三倍节点的 kubernetes 集群的成本不会增加 3 倍，但租用一个节点的成本却会增加 3 倍。对于无服务器来说更是如此。

我可以想象很多小型应用程序在无服务器确实是最具成本效益的解决方案时购买无服务器，然后它们陷入困境，因为无服务器平台很容易将自己锁定。

Cloud is more cost effective the less of it you have because it doesn’t cost 3x more to maintain a kubernetes cluster with thrice the nodes, but it does cost 3x more to rent one. This is even more true for serverless.

I can imagine a lot of small apps buy into serverless at a time where it’s legitimately the most cost-effective solution and then they’re stuck because serverless platforms are easy to lock yourself into.

在 HN 中查看
Imustaskforhelp 2026/04/10 00:31
> 这就是他们获胜的地方，人们认为 AWS/GCP/Azure 必须是默认的，而实际上，实际需要能够快速扩展/缩减的平台数量可能低于所有平台的 1%。大多数平台可以通过适当的专用硬件来节省资金并运行得更好，而不是默认使用云。

这。我有点希望更多的人知道这件事。而且即使是 1% 也可能太大了。我的意思是，Lichess 实际上拥有数百万人（如果不是更多的话），这绝对在 0.001% 的群体之内。

我有点希望将来在这个领域做点什么，我确实觉得只是人们不知道而已。我一直在考虑接触一些公司，告诉他们如果迁移并使用开源解决方案和这些 dedi 服务器并在这些 dedi/vps 之上进行设置，他们可以节省多少费用。

我一直在考虑（在未来）联系几家公司，让他们从迁移中节省净钱，同时每月向他们收取几百美元，我可以选择极少数的公司（比如 15-20 家），以获得足够的钱，这样我就可以吃炸薯条并管理他们的服务器！

对于除了 AWS/GCP/Azure 之外的每个人来说，这都是一种双赢的局面，他们希望表明可扩展性很难等等，而对于大多数（如果不是本质上*所有）企业来说，这是一个错误的前提。

就我个人而言，我也提到诸如 slack 之类的东西，我不明白为什么人们可能会想要 slack，当像矩阵这样的东西存在并且可以通过适当的 3-2-1 备份安全地自托管时，对于大多数意图和目的来说，即使不比 slack 更好，实际上也是好的。

对我来说，虽然对此有点担心，但我不确定如果我为他们设置这些服务器是否有充分根据，现在我希望设置它们，以便它们的错误尽可能少，但如果公司开始认为我什么也没做，然后在我将它们设置在这些专用服务器上后他们停止支付我的合同怎么办？我想我希望他们相信人类支持的价值，我想我也有点不确定在哪里可以找到这样的企业。

我的兄弟兼职做一些自由职业，我问他这些事情，他提到他大部分都必须使用 AWS，我提到为什么不使用 dedi，他说他做了他被要求做的事情，而该公司希望他使用 AWS，所以他使用 AWS，所以我想在这种情况下，我需要至少有兴趣对专用服务器更加开放的公司。

我确信会有公司对这一切感兴趣，我也有兴趣为他们做事，但我不确定连接两者的中间部分。我真的很想听听您对这一切的想法，祝您有美好的一天！

> That's where they won, people think AWS/GCP/Azure has to be the default while in reality, the number of platforms that actually need to be able to scale up/down fast are probably below 1% of all platforms out there. Most platforms would save money and run better with proper dedicated hardware rather than going for clouds by default.

This. I kind of wish if more people knew about it. Also even 1% can be too big. I mean Lichess is literally having millions of people if not more, It's definitely within the 0.001% group.

I kind of wish to do something in this space in the future, I do feel like its just that people don't know about it. I have been thinking to approach some companies and just tell them how much they can save if they migrate and use open source solutions and these dedi servers and setting things on top of these dedi's/vps's.

I have been thinking of (within future), to contact a few companies and to actually have them save net money from migration while charging them a few hundred bucks a month and I can just have a very handful selection of companies (say 15-20) to have enough money so that I can eat french fries and manage their servers!

It feels a win-win-win situation for everyone except AWS/GCP/Azure who wish to suggest that scalability is hard etc. and this false premise for most if not essentially* all businesses.

Personally, I am also saying things like slack for example, I don't understand why people might want slack when things like matrix exist and can be self-hosted securely with proper 3-2-1 backups and for most intents and purposes is actually good if not better than slack.

To me, a bit of concern though with this and I am not sure if it is well-founded is what if I set these servers for them, now I will wish to set them up so that they have as little errors as possible but what if the companies start to think that I am doing nothing and then they stop paying my contracts after I have set them up on these dedicated. I guess I hope that they believe in the value of human support and I guess I am also a bit unsure of where do I find such businesses are.

My brother does some freelancing on the side and I ask him these things and he mentions that mostly he has to use AWS, I mention why not dedi and he says that he does what he is asked to do and that company wants him to use AWS so he uses AWS, so I guess within this context, I need companies who are atleast interested in being a bit more open about thinking about dedicated servers.

I am sure that there would be companies interested in all of this and I am interested in doing things for them but I am not sure about the middle part of connecting the two. I would be genuinely interested to hear your thoughts on all of this and have a nice day emsh!

在 HN 中查看
lxgr 2026/04/10 01:27
它似乎也比 chess.com 具有更好的可用性，在 chess.com 上，我经常遇到游戏突然结束并从我的游戏历史记录中完全删除的情况，因为我只能假设服务器重新启动。
It also seems to have significantly better availability than chess.com, where I regularly had games end abruptly and be completely removed from my game history due to what I can only assume is a server restart.
在 HN 中查看
chrisco23 2026/04/10 00:58
爱巫妖。 这些天我玩的不多，只是看国际象棋直播解说。 因此，当我看到 Take Take Take 周一举办了发布会但周二没有直播（被 chess.com 抛弃）时，我感到很惊讶。 我从来没有在手机上下棋，但我很好奇 Take Take Take 如何将法学硕士纳入英语语言解释。 去年秋天，我对此进行了某种概念验证，但并没有像 TTT 应用程序那样具体化。

于是我掸掉一台旧安卓平板电脑上的灰尘，开始玩一款游戏。 很高兴看到我直接登录了我的 lichess 帐户，玩了 10 + 5 未评级的游戏，并进行了游戏评论。 我认为这对周围的每个人来说都是一件好事，正如其他人所表达的那样，我希望 lichess 不要陷入一些商业悲伤之中。 游戏评论并不是惊天动地的，而是对每一步的详细解释，我认为这会对很多玩家，尤其是新玩家有所帮助。

我会坚持在 43 英寸电视显示器上在浏览器中玩 lichess，运行并阅读本地 Stockfish 评估，无需英文解释。

Love Lichess. These days I haven't been playing very much but always watching chess streaming commentary. So I was surprised when I saw Take Take Take had a launch party Monday but no stream on Tuesday (getting dumped by chess.com). I never play chess on a phone but I was curious to see how Take Take Take might be incorporating LLM for English language explanation. Last fall I did a sort of proof of concept of this, not nearly fleshed out like the TTT app.

So I literally dusted off an old Android tablet and played one game. Pleased to see I got logged right in to my lichess account, played a 10 + 5 unrated, did game review. I think this should be great for everybody all around, and as others have expressed I hope lichess doesn't get caught up in some business grief. The game review was not earth-shattering but decent move-by-move explanation that I think will help a lot of players, especially newer players.

I will stick to playing on lichess in browser, on a 43" tv monitor, running and reading local Stockfish eval., without the English explan.

在 HN 中查看
harlequinetcie 2026/04/10 00:03
巫妖，你们太棒了。

最重要的是，随着软件工程世界中发生的一切，我将国际象棋视为我们在过去几十年中看到的一个地方。 Lichess 是其中的重要组成部分。

我希望这笔交易能带来两件事： (1)让更多的人接触国际象棋， (2) 实际上，帮助 Lichess 找到一种方法来奖励那些在其中工作的人应有的报酬。

继续出色的工作，

Lichess, you guys rock.

Above all, with everything that's happening in the software engineering world rn, I look at Chess as a place were we've seen it play out in the past decades. And Lichess is a big part of that.

I hope this deal helps two things: (1) Bring more people to Chess, (2) Actually, help Lichess find out a way to reward those working in it as much as they deserve.

Keep on the amazing work,

在 HN 中查看
vkr2020 2026/04/10 01:15
Love lichess - 我下载了他们的国际象棋谜题数据库，并为我的儿子构建了一个简单的应用程序来学习 mate-in-3 谜题。他们太棒了，值得所有的爱！
Love lichess - I downloaded their chess puzzle database and built a simple app for my son to learn mate-in-3 puzzles. They are amazing and deserve all the love!
在 HN 中查看
elicash 2026/04/10 00:47
对于任何在 Netflix 上看过关于马格努斯与汉斯的戏剧的新国际象棋纪录片的人来说，你都会记得马格努斯和他的父亲在 chess.com 上有多么生气，这就是让我对这次合作感兴趣的原因。 （Magnus 是 TTT 联合创始人）
For anybody who watched the new chess documentary on Netflix about the Magnus vs Hans drama, you'll remember how pissed Magnus and his dad are at chess.com, which is what makes this partnership interesting to me. (Magnus is a TTT cofounder)

tartoran 2026/04/09 23:31
这里是 Lichess 的忠实粉丝。如此出色且被低估的服务。
Big fan of Lichess here. Such a great and underrated service.
在 HN 中查看
ddp26 2026/04/09 22:33
免费开源模式确实有其竞争优势！
The free open source model does have its competitive advantages!
在 HN 中查看
TheSilva 2026/04/09 22:14
这是巨大的，恭喜巫妖团队。
This is huge, congrats to the lichess team.
在 HN 中查看
k2xl 2026/04/09 23:32
Lichess 绝对是一个非常棒的平台。作为一名国际象棋爱好者，作为我和其他一些人正在构建的国际象棋网站的工程师（无耻的插件， https://chess67.com），它们是我使用过的唯一一个可以通过 API 轻松访问这么多内容的平台。

他们的 Oauth 需要特殊的应用程序注册或任何 Oauth 秘密 - 我见过的唯一平台可以做到这一点。

我确实想知道这如何让人们能够将 Lichess 的玩家池集成到他们自己的应用程序中。

Lichess has been absolutely fantastic platform. AS a chess enthusiast as an engineer of a chess website me and some others are building (shameless plug, https://chess67.com), they are the only platform I have worked with where so much is so easily accessible in terms of their APIs.

Their Oauth requires to special app registration nor any oauth secrets - only platform I have seen that does that.

I do wonder how this opens up ability for people to integrate Lichess’ player pool to their own apps.

在 HN 中查看
CarterWilliams 2026/04/10 01:25
是的，这是一个非常有趣的观点。开放性是 Lichess 对于组织者和开发者来说如此强大的主要原因。

从我作为教练和俱乐部组织者的角度来看，chess67 看起来很有趣，特别是对于举办锦标赛和为我的教练和活动获得曝光而言。

但我确实想知道长期的界限在哪里。如果更多的工具开始利用玩家池，那么在保持开放和防止人们搭便车之间可能会取得平衡 Lichess 生态系统。

不管怎样，它都是非常独特的。在国际象棋世界的其他地方你看不到这种程度的可及性。

Yeah that’s a really interesting point. The openness is mainly what makes Lichess so powerful for organizers and developers.

chess67 looks interesting from my perspective as a coach and club organizer, especially for running tournaments and gaining exposure for my coaching and events.

But I do wonder where the boundary is long term. If more tools start tapping into the player pool, there’s probably a balance between staying open and preventing people from just free riding on the Lichess ecosystem.

Either way, it’s pretty unique. You don’t really see that level of accessibility elsewhere in the chess world.

在 HN 中查看
dwa3592 2026/04/10 02:11
我来这里只是想说 Lichess 太棒了。爱它。
Just came here to say that Lichess is amazing. Love it.
在 HN 中查看
NickC25 2026/04/10 00:24
爱巫妖。

我喜欢 chess.com 的唯一一点是能够创建自定义变体、编辑它们并将它们释放到野外。 最近很喜欢迷你屋，这是一个很酷的变体。

我很想看到 Lichess 添加 bughouse，因为它的表弟 Pychess 最近首次推出了它，而且看起来效果很好。 Chess.com 有精神病院。

Love Lichess.

The only thing I love about chess.com is the ability to create custom variants, edit them, and unleash them into the wild. Been loving minihouse lately, such a cool variant.

Would love to see Lichess add bughouse, as its cousin Pychess recently debuted it and it seems to work fine. Chess.com has bughouse.

在 HN 中查看
ForHackernews 2026/04/09 22:39
恰好，“Take Take Take签署合作协议”也是OpenAI在出版业的惯用伎俩。
As it happens "Take Take Take Sign Cooperation Agreement" is also OpenAI's modus operandi when it comes to the publishing industry.
在 HN 中查看
